messages.content: No connection adapters were found for '10.102.209.14:8000/v1/chat/completions'

# Error: No Connection Adapters Found

This error occurs when a URL is missing its protocol scheme (http:// or https://). Python's `requests` library needs to know which protocol to use.

## Solution

Add the protocol scheme to your URL:

```python
# ❌ Wrong - Missing protocol
url = "10.102.209.14:8000/v1/chat/completions"

# ✅ Correct - With HTTP
url = "http://10.102.209.14:8000/v1/chat/completions"

# ✅ Or with HTTPS (if using SSL)
url = "https://10.102.209.14:8000/v1/chat/completions"
```

## Complete Example

```python
import requests

# Correct URL format
url = "http://10.102.209.14:8000/v1/chat/completions"

headers = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

data = {
    "model": "your-model",
    "messages": [
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}
    ]
}

response = requests.post(url, json=data, headers=headers)
print(response.json())
```

## Quick Fix

If you're using this in your code, simply prepend `http://` or `https://`:

```python
# If your variable looks like this:
base_url = "10.102.209.14:8000"
endpoint = f"{base_url}/v1/chat/completions"

# Change to:
base_url = "http://10.102.209.14:8000"
endpoint = f"{base_url}/v1/chat/completions"
```

**Note:** Since this is a local IP address (10.x.x.x), you'll most likely want to use `http://` unless the server is specifically configured with SSL/TLS certificates.
